category_title: Students with ASD who struggle with emotional regulation and unstructured social interaction
minimal_context: The student is asked to transition from his desk to the carpet for a group discussion during normal class time.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our group discussion. Please come sit on the carpet with the other children.

STUDENT: (Stays seated at his desk) I am going to sit at my desk.

TUTOR: Everyone else is congregated around me on the carpet. You need to join us.

STUDENT: I don't like the disorder and clutter when everyone sits together.

TUTOR: We have to do the discussion now, so please come over here.

STUDENT: (Becomes angry) No! I am not moving! I am staying at my desk!

options.A: Joke with the student and laugh at oneself to lighten the mood about transitioning to the carpet for the group discussion.
options.B: Have the student draw a comic strip or write a creative story at his desk instead of joining the group discussion.
options.C: Teach a replacement behavior for when the student is asked to join the group discussion on the carpet with the other children.
options.D: Arrange the children in a circle on the carpet while remaining outside the circle to observe and manage the sequence of speakers.
